title: elymus canadensis var. glaucifolius
intro: Elymus canadensis var. glaucifolius, also known as blue wildrye, is a type of perennial grass native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant has been used to treat a variety of health issues, including fever, colds, and skin conditions, due to its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Blue wildrye can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use, and is also used in traditional crafts and landscaping due to its attractive foliage and ability to stabilize soil.
